name: Kabbalah
shortDescription: Kabbalah is an esoteric method, discipline, and school of thought in Jewish mysticism. It is a set of esoteric teachings meant to explain the relationship between an unchanging, eternal, and mysterious Ein Sof (infinity) and the mortal and finite universe (his creation).
description: Kabbalah, also spelled Cabala, Cabbala, or Kaballah, is a body of esoteric Jewish teachings aimed at understanding the nature of God and the universe, and ultimately, achieving spiritual enlightenment. It's a complex system of thought that interprets the Hebrew Bible through symbolism and allegory. In the context of Tarot, Kabbalah provides a framework for understanding the structure and meaning of the cards, particularly the Major Arcana and the Tree of Life. The Tree of Life, with its ten Sephirot (emanations of God) and 22 paths, is often mapped onto the Tarot deck, with each card corresponding to a specific Sephira or path. The Major Arcana are often seen as representing the 22 letters of the Hebrew alphabet, which are also associated with the paths on the Tree of Life. Through this association, the Tarot can be used as a tool for exploring Kabbalistic concepts and achieving a deeper understanding of the self and the divine. While Jewish Kabbalah originated independently of the Tarot, its integration into Western esoteric traditions, particularly Hermeticism, has made it a central component of many Tarot systems. This integration emphasizes the interconnectedness of all things and the potential for spiritual growth through the study of sacred symbols and texts. The influence of Kabbalah on the Tarot extends to various aspects, including card meanings, spreads, and the overall philosophy behind its use. The Tree of Life, in particular, provides a structured framework for interpreting the cards and understanding their relationships to one another. The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn played a significant role in popularizing the Kabbalistic Tarot, further solidifying its place in modern esoteric practices.
